'''Mickey Higham''' (born 18 September 1980) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|hk}} in the 1990s, 2000s, and 2010s.

He played for the [[Leigh Centurions]] in three separate spells in the [[Super League]] and [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. Higham also played for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]], [[Wigan Warriors]] and the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the top flight. He played for both [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] and [[England national rugby league team|England]] at international level.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Background==
Higham was born in [[Higher End|Billinge Higher End]], [[Metropolitan Borough of Wigan|Wigan]], [[England]].

==Playing career==
===Leigh Centurions===
Higham joined [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] from [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] in November 2000.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.lancashiretelegraph.co.uk/news/6056626.higham-joins-saints/|title = Higham joins Saints| work=Lancashire Telegraph | date=24 November 2000 }}</ref>

===St Helens===
Higham played for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] from the interchange bench in their [[2002 Super League Grand Final]] victory against [[Bradford Bulls]]. Having won [[Super League VI]], St Helens contested the [[2003 World Club Challenge]] against 2002 NRL Premiers, [[Sydney Roosters]]. Higham played from the interchange bench in Saints' 38–0 loss.

He suffered an ankle injury which ruled him out of most of 2004's [[Super League IX]]. He'd made over 100 appearances for St Helens, scoring 33 tries.

===Wigan Warriors===
Higham joined [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in October 2005 after a transfer deal involving three clubs, and fellow {{rlp|hk}} [[Terry Newton]]. Higham was unable to join Wigan directly as his club, [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]], would not sell.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.totalrl.com/five-ex-super-league-players-who-bizarrely-never-played-a-game-for-the-club-they-signed-for/ | title=Five ex-Super League players who bizarrely never played a game for the club they signed for | first=Callum | last=Walker | date=24 January 2025 | access-date=25 January 2025 | publisher=Total Rugby League}}</ref> However, [[Bradford Bulls]] stepped in and bought Higham from Saints for £70,000 and then immediately released him to Wigan. In exchange, Wigan allowed [[Terry Newton]] to join Bradford.<ref>{{cite news | url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2005/oct/21/rugbyleague.sport | title=Bulls quick to trade Higham for Newton | first=Gareth | last=Walker | work=The Guardian | date=21 October 2005 | access-date=25 January 2025}}</ref> Higham signed a three-year deal with Wigan and commented: "It looked at one stage as though I would be playing next season at Bradford but when the opportunity to join Wigan came up at the last minute I jumped at it."  At Wigan, Higham again teamed up with coach [[Ian Millward]] who he had played under at both Leigh and St Helens. Millward was however sacked by Wigan in April 2006 and Higham, ironically, was coached by [[Brian Noble (rugby league)|Brian Noble]] who was Bradford coach at the time of the "three-club transfer". Higham's contract at Wigan was not renewed, and he subsequently signed for [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] on a two-year deal.

===Warrington Wolves===
Higham enjoyed a successful spell with the Wolves making 174 appearances and was a fans favourite throughout his time at the club.<ref name="Leeds 6-30 Warrington">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/8938067.stm|title=Leeds 6–30 Warrington|publisher=BBC|date=28 August 2010|access-date=28 August 2010|first=Ged|last=Scott}}</ref>

Higham played in the [[2010 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Leeds 6-30 Warrington" /><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2010/aug/28/leeds-rhinos-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final |location=London |work=The Guardian |title=Chris Hicks hat-trick leads Warrington to Challenge Cup triumph over Leeds |first=Andy |last=Wilson |date=28 August 2010 |access-date=22 November 2019<!--DASHBot--> |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100831105934/http://www.guardian.co.uk/sport/2010/aug/28/leeds-rhinos-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final |archive-date=31 August 2010 |url-status=live |df=dmy }}</ref>

He played in the [[2012 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2012/aug/25/leeds-warrington-challenge-cup |title=Warrington's battered Brett Hodgson recovers to see off Leeds in final|date=25 August 2012|work=Guardian UK|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref><ref name="theguardian_com">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2012/oct/06/warrington-leeds-grand-final|title=Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington|publisher=Guardian|date=6 October 2012|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/19362692|title=Leeds Rhinos 18–35 Warrington Wolves|date=25 August 2012|work=BBC Sport|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web
 |url=http://www.warringtonwolves.org/warrington-wolves-are-challenge-cup-winners-2012-5628 
 |title=Warrington Wolves Are Challenge Cup Winners 2012! 
 |date=25 August 2012 
 |work=Warrington Wolves Official Site 
 |access-date=20 November 2019
 |url-status=dead 
 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131217230118/http://www.warringtonwolves.org/warrington-wolves-are-challenge-cup-winners-2012-5628 
 |archive-date=17 December 2013 
 |df=dmy 
}}</ref>

He played in the [[2012 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite news|title=Grand Final: Warrington 18-26 Leeds|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/19805055|access-date=20 November 2019|newspaper=BBC Sport|date=6 October 2012}}</ref><ref name="theguardian_com" />

He played in the [[2013 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by the [[Wigan Warriors]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite web|title=Super League Grand Final |url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/super_league/grandfinal |publisher=Super League |access-date=14 July 2013 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130729050226/http://www.superleague.co.uk/super_league/grandfinal |archive-date=29 July 2013}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|last=Newsum|first=Matt|title=Super League Grand Final: Wigan Warriors beat Warrington|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/24384930|access-date=13 November 2019|newspaper=BBC Sport|date=5 October 2013}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|title=Super League Grand Final: Warrington v Wigan|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/0/rugby-league/24339698|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131007163603/http://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/0/rugby-league/24339698|url-status=dead|archive-date=7 October 2013|work=BBC Sport|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/10358228/Super-League-Grand-Final-2013-Warrington-Wolves-16-Wigan-Warriors-30-match-report.html|title= Warrington Wolves 16 Wigan Warriors 30|date=5 October 2013|work=Daily Telegraph|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2013/oct/05/warrington-wigan-super-league-grand-final|title= Wigan see off Warrington in X-rated Grand Final to complete double|date=5 October 2013|work=Guardian |access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref>

===Leigh===
Higham then returned to his first club [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] for £50,000 on 20 May 2015.<ref name="Higham signs for Leigh Centurions">{{cite news|url=http://www.leighreporter.co.uk/sport/leigh-centurions/higham-signs-for-centurions-1-7269966|title=Higham signs for Leigh Centurions|publisher=Leigh Reporter|date=20 May 2015|access-date=20 May 2015|first=Mark|last=Taylor}}{{Dead link|date=May 2025 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }}</ref>

On 11 September 2019 Higham announced his retirement to join the Leigh coaching staff<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.leighjournal.co.uk/news/17894713.micky-higham-retire-end-season/|title=Micky Higham to retire at end of season|date=11 September 2019 |publisher=Leigh Journal|access-date=19 Dec 2020}}</ref>

===International career===
He made his [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] début in the [[2004 Gillette Tri-Nations]] against [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]], after recovering from an ankle injury which ruled him out of most of 2004's [[Super League IX]].

In September 2008, he was named in the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#England|England training squad]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]],<ref name="Myler gets England call">{{cite web|url=http://www.englandrl.co.uk/article.php?id=11890|title=Myler gets England call|publisher=England Rugby League|date=29 September 2008|access-date=3 October 2008|url-status=unfit|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20081007232803/http://www.englandrl.co.uk/article.php?id=11890|archive-date=7 October 2008 }}</ref> and in October 2008 he was named in the final 24-man [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#England|England]] squad.<ref name="Purdham earns World Cup call-up">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7656553.stm|title=Purdham earns World Cup call-up|publisher=BBC|date=7 October 2008|access-date=7 October 2008}}</ref> He was named in the [[England national rugby league team|England]] team to face [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] at the [[Keepmoat Stadium]], [[Doncaster]], prior to England's departure for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="Gleeson to lead new-look England">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7642490.stm|title=Gleeson to lead new-look England|publisher=BBC|date=9 October 2008|access-date=10 October 2008}}</ref> He made his [[England national rugby league team|England]] début in the victory over [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] on 10 October 2008.<ref name="England warm up in style">{{cite news|url=http://www.skysports.com/story/0,19528,12196_4297504,00.html|title=England warm up in style|publisher=Sky Sports|date=10 October 2008|access-date=11 October 2008}}</ref>
